hawkish_phrases: policy rates would remain at current or lower levels for an extended period of time
structural reforms... boost job creation and increase growth potential
fiscal policy could play a role in supporting the economic recovery
a limited period of overshooting in future
there was no limit to how far it was willing to deploy instruments within its mandate
dovish_phrases: downside risks had increased again amid heightened uncertainty
inflation dynamics had continued to be weaker than expected
increased the risk of second-round effects
review and possibly reconsider the monetary policy stance at the next monetary policy meeting in early March
willing and able to take further monetary policy action, if warranted
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held rates unchanged but signaled a strong easing bias by stating it would review and possibly reconsider the monetary policy stance at the March 2016 meeting, reaffirming forward guidance that rates would remain at current or lower levels for an extended period amid increased downside risks and weaker-than-expected inflation.
